question_frequency.rationale: The Tutor never asks the student didactic questions. All questions originate from the student; the Tutor only delivers explanations and tables. There are essentially zero teachable-moment prompts initiated by the Tutor.

question_quality.rationale: Since the Tutor doesn't pose questions, this can only be judged by the conceptual forks it surfaces. It does highlight the simultaneous-update issue and clamping vs. skipping, which are central, but never frames them as choices for the student to reason through.

scaffolding.rationale: The progression is student-driven and explanations are clear, but there is no Tutor-led scaffolding. The Tutor solves the whole thing upfront and then answers follow-ups, which is the opposite of guided discovery.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm, explanations are concrete with good tables and a strong in-place mutation walkthrough. Conversation flows naturally, though it's one-directional (Tutor lectures, student asks).

effectiveness.rationale: (pinned to 3 — this is the baseline reference run)

friction.rationale: (pinned to 3 — this is the baseline reference run)
